name: Mamta
description_short: MAMTA Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, nonprofit financial institution serving employees of Mamaroneck and Rye Neck school districts and their families with personal loans, share accounts, and educational financial support.
description_long:
MAMTA Federal Credit Union was established to serve the financial needs of employees from the Mamaroneck and Rye Neck school districts and their families. As a federal credit union, it operates as a nonprofit, member-owned cooperative where account holders are both customers and owners with voting rights on the board of directors using a one-person, one-vote system regardless of account balance. The institution is chartered and supervised by the federal government and maintains NCUA insurance protection for member deposits.

MAMTA offers core credit union services including personal loans at reasonable interest rates, share accounts, and share certificates. As a member of the Education Credit Union Council (ECUC), the credit union provides special loan rates specifically for educational purposes. The institution positions itself as a neighborhood-oriented financial organization that combines personalized, confidential service with the protections and resources typically associated with larger financial institutions. Members benefit from the cooperative structure where profits are returned to members rather than external shareholders.

What distinguishes MAMTA is its deep community commitment and focus on educational support. Beyond standard lending, the credit union annually contributes to multiple educational scholarship funds within the Mamaroneck and Rye Neck communities, including the Alice Lampen Scholarship for Rye Neck students pursuing business education and the Eugene A. Talgo Scholarship for Mamaroneck High School students. The institution also provides yearly financial assistance to needy families in its service area, demonstrating a community-centered mission beyond profit maximization.

Prospective members should note that MAMTA operates with significant limitations. The credit union only serves a restricted membership—employees of two specific school districts and their families—making it inaccessible to the general public. Office hours are limited (7:30am-3:30pm weekdays, closed Fridays in summer), the institution closes during school holidays, and it no longer accepts cash deposits, requiring members to use alternative deposit methods. These constraints make MAMTA practical only for eligible employees seeking a community-focused, mission-driven financial institution.
services: Personal loans at reasonable interest rates
Share accounts (savings accounts for credit union members)
Educational loans through ECUC special rates program
Member financial counseling and personal assistance
Alice Lampen Scholarship program for Rye Neck students
Eugene A. Talgo Scholarship for Mamaroneck High School students
Annual financial assistance stipends to needy families
Federal credit union charter and NCUA insurance protection
pros: Member-owned nonprofit structure with one-person, one-vote governance regardless of account size
Special educational loan rates through Education Credit Union Council (ECUC) membership
Annual scholarships and community financial assistance programs (Alice Lampen Scholarship, Eugene A. Talgo Scholarship, Larchmont-Mamaroneck Student Aid Fund)
Personalized, confidential member service with neighborhood-based relationship focus
Federal credit union status with NCUA insurance protection on deposits
Reasonable interest rates on personal loans compared to traditional banks
No requirement to maximize profits—surplus returns to members rather than shareholders
cons: Extremely limited membership—only employees of Mamaroneck and Rye Neck school districts and their families can join (not open to general public)
Restricted office hours (7:30am-3:30pm Mon-Thu, 7:30am-3pm Fri during school year; 8:30am-12:30pm Mon-Thu summer only) and closures during school breaks
No longer accepts cash deposits, requiring members to use alternative deposit methods
Limited service menu appears to focus primarily on personal loans and share accounts without mention of credit cards, mortgages, or other products
Small institution with fewer resources and technology capabilities compared to larger credit unions or banks
best_for: Employees of Mamaroneck or Rye Neck school districts seeking personalized financial services
Families of school district employees pursuing educational loans or student financial support
Members prioritizing community-focused, nonprofit financial institutions with scholarship programs
Individuals seeking reasonable-rate personal loans within a member-owned cooperative structure
diagnosis: MAMTA Federal Credit Union is best for employees of Mamaroneck or Rye Neck school districts and their families who value community-focused, mission-driven banking with educational support and member-ownership. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to school district employees and families—the general public cannot join—and the institution has limited hours with closures during school breaks and no longer accepts cash deposits.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Mamta — NCUA-insured credit union in Mamaroneck, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
